name: Wilmot Collins
subject: Celebrating America
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
body:
#
Team,
243 years ago, a group of visionaries declared their independence and created a country where my story would be possible. I took an oath to defend it and the Constitution that governs it like so many before me. Last week, the Fourth of July was a yearly reminder to live up to that oath.
[****](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190709&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
And there is no better place to spend the Fourth of July than Butte, America. We marched in the annual parade with our supporters--and I was so happy that my wife Maddie was able to join. I enjoyed shaking hands, taking selfies, and giving out what seemed like hundreds of fist bumps to the kids along the route. And of course, I thanked every veteran I saw for their service.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190709&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
On July 5th, we attended the Arlee Pow-Wow on the Flathead Reservation where we danced and celebrated with new friends and old, including Anna Whiting-Sorrell and Gene Sorrell, a fellow veteran and friend who I joined to help elect Senator Jon Tester several years ago.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190709&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Despite a torrential downpour and even some hail, Team Wilmot also enjoyed marching in Livingston's annual July 4th parade. Afterward, we attended a BBQ with the Park County Democrats and spoke with them about the issues that are most important to their families.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190709&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
**[We are working hard to travel all over Montana and visit people in every corner of this state, but we need your support to keep gas in the tank. Will you contribute $5 now?](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190709&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
I'm so thankful for your continued support--and hope to see you soon on the campaign trail!
Thank you,
Wilmot Collins

**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190709&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

* * *

---

Friend,

_**President Trump is right.**_

It's totally ridiculous that our Nation's government cannot ask a basic question of citizenship in our very EXPENSIVE, very important Census.

The American People deserve to know who is in this country, Friend, and there's only one way to find out:
**We need to ask every person _' ARE YOU AN AMERICAN CITIZEN, YES OR NO?'_**
The President wants to know if you're on his side. All you have to do is answer one simple question:

**Do you believe the 2020 United States Census should ask if people are American citizens? **

**[ABSOLUTELY!](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=e9edf39fd0d2902cd20e2e806ef64d6337b81f6ed0f8079f26ec76612ac4071a8f0a3e845d697bd876af682b8ae385bd37e5d8cd2d27b53b)**

In another era, this would be COMMON SENSE... but the _**Left**_ is trying to block President Trump's efforts to ask people if they are citizens of this great Nation.

We can't Keep America Great for all Americans if we don't know who's in this country.

**[Please take our Official 2020 Census Survey NOW to let the President know your thoughts.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=e9edf39fd0d2902c38701a7fb0a1096c7af92608e2e2f8bee10ceced6908555eb63b88f8bf7ed79ff3d89af1759208cbe91e092427e2fc8f)**

Breaking News---Virginia Ranked 1 for Business
| | | [](https://act.ralphnortham.com/page/m/296f21cc/39260142/42e036ec/62639fe5/4245222261/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/twa19")
---
Friends-- Over the past 18 months, we've worked together to continue our progress, making Virginia a Commonwealth that everyone wants to live, work, and raise a family in. _**That 's why I'm so proud to share with you that CNBC has just returned Virginia to it's rightful place -for the first time in a decade---by naming us the best state in the nation to do business.**_ [You can see my announcement on CNBC here.](https://act.ralphnortham.com/page/m/296f21cc/39260142/42e036ec/62639fe4/4245222261/VEsE/ "https://www.cnbc.com") We accomplished this by:
* Increasing teacher pay and investing in our public schools
* Investing in the best in the nation workforce
* Expanding Medicaid for approximately 400,000 Virginians
* Making record investments in broadband and affordable housing
* Signing my first executive order upon taking office to ban discrimination so Virginia is welcoming to all
* And last week, announcing a historic initiative to increase opportunity for small, women, and minority-owned businesses

It's these core values that make us the Commonwealth we all love-- our commitment to public education, inclusiveness, healthy families, and our drive to ensure every Virginian has a chance to succeed, no matter who you are or where you live. Thank you for all you do as we forge the way ahead. **Ralph Northam**

This election cycle, most campaigns host big fundraisers with this number in mind: $2,800. That's the maximum one person can contribute to a primary campaign.

In those rooms, the focus is on getting a few wealthy donors to shell out as much as they can.

But our campaign is a movement, and we're thinking differently. We're thinking about $28. That was our average contribution in Q2, and it added up to $19.1 million.

How does $28 compete with $2,800? Well, it works when over 300,000 people donate for the first time. That's exactly what happened in Q2, and that's how we need to keep building this movement.

**We 're so grateful that so many people have invested in this campaign for the first time. It's a powerfully important sign of momentum. Pundits and reporters will be watching to see if we can keep these numbers up... | | | "" This election cycle, most campaigns host big fundraisers with this number in mind: $2,800. That's the maximum one person can contribute to a primary campaign. In those rooms, the focus is on getting a few wealthy donors to shell out as much as they can. But our campaign is a movement, and we're thinking differently. We're thinking about $28. That was our average contribution in Q2, and it added up to $19.1 million. How does $28 compete with $2,800? Well, it works when over 300,000 people donate for the first time. That's exactly what happened in Q2, and that's how we need to keep building this movement. We 're so grateful that so many people have invested in this campaign for the first time. It's a powerfully important sign of momentum. subject: From the Trail
body:
**

Good morning Peter,
------------------------------------------------------------

Over the last few weeks, Marc and his staff have been out knocking on doors, asking neighbors for their votes, and listening to hear what he can do to make things better for them.

In June, we knocked on Juan's door. He told us that he had never had a candidate come to his house before. He was so moved by our knocking on his door, listening to his opinions, and caring what he had to say that he wrote us a message. He wrote in saying that it was so meaningful that someone came and asked for his and his family's support.

Too many of our neighbors feel like they are voiceless and powerless in elections, and so they choose not to vote at all. We need to let people like Juan know that their voices are heard, and that their vote matters! Donate $40 today (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=526c1e8e6b-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_07_09_03_32&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-526c1e8e6b-32267927) so that Marc can purchase 100 campaign cards to tell people who he is.

In this country, in this community, in our district, every vote matters!

Donate! (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=526c1e8e6b-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_07_09_03_32&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-526c1e8e6b-32267927)
Visiting voters in Acres Homes' Juneteenth Parade.

Marc out in the community.

It takes a team to win an election, and your support means everything to us. Reply to this email or send an email volunteer@marcfloresforcongress.com (mailto:volunteer@marcfloresforcongress.com?subject=RE%3A%20Volunteering) to find out how you can support us at future events. If you or someone you know would like to be considered for our internship program, send your resume to that same email!

Thank you for all your support thus far! Be sure to follow us on social media for all of the latest updates on the campaign.

Best wishes,

Marc Flores

name: Tim Kaine
subject: [ADD YOUR NAME] Virginia Republicans adjourned the special session without taking up any legislation. That's shameful. Will you sign my petition?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Derek:

Yesterday, Virginia had the opportunity to take meaningful steps to reduce gun violence. Our lawmakers could have come together to pass commonsense reforms. Instead, Republicans voted to adjourn the special legislative session after less than two hours of debate, and declined to take up a single piece of legislation. _That's shameful._

[**Add your name to my petition if you agree that Virginians deserve better.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f177a/3e0906/7887caf4/3e309373/2401265956/VEsH/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/enough-is-enough-gun-violence?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+to+my+petition+if+you+agre&utm_campaign=20190709_1_PET_TK_VASpecialSession&source=20190709_1_PET_TK_VASpecialSession")

After the horrific shooting in Virginia Beach, I spent time talking with families out in the community about gun violence. I wanted to find out what they were thinking and feeling. Here's one of the heartbreaking stories I heard: An elementary school teacher in Charlottesville told me that she keeps a file cabinet by her classroom door so she can knock it over to block an active shooter from entering. When her school has lockdown drills, she stands against the door so that if shots are fired, the bullets would hit her first.

**How on earth can we hear things like this and still refuse to make our communities safer? I'm deeply disappointed that after the Governor brought our state leaders back to the Capitol for the express purpose of taking action, Republicans in the General Assembly dug in their heels and played party politics instead.**

**To put politics ahead of people's lives is reprehensible.** [**Add your name to say enough is enough.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f177a/3e0906/7887caf4/3e309372/2401265956/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/enough-is-enough-gun-violence?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+say+enough+is+enough&utm_campaign=20190709_1_PET_TK_VASpecialSession&source=20190709_1_PET_TK_VASpecialSession")

Thank you for standing with me.

-Tim Kaine

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: We Can't Afford to Delay Passage of USMCA Trade Deal
body:
"China would love nothing more than for Congress to kill the USMCA and stall future trade agreements..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=474e17f3b6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **We Can't Afford to Delay Passage of USMCA Trade Deal**

“China would love nothing more than for Congress to kill the USMCA and stall future trade agreements, so it can promote its own low standards and coercive economic agenda with other countries,” Rep. Michael McCaul (R-TX) writes in _The Hill_.

“The United States has a strategic interest in writing the future rules for trade that support U.S. competitiveness and opens markets to our goods, services and values. Passing the USMCA would kickstart a wave of bipartisan trade wins for the United States that would grow our economy and advance our interests and national security. I urge my Democratic colleagues not to delay bringing this bill forward for a vote as soon as it is sent to Congress.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9bc5983be3&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| President Trump’s “promise of putting American workers and interests first in negotiating trade deals resonated both with the Republican base and with Democrats who felt their party had abandoned them. Making sure our international trading partners were treating us fairly was a chief campaign promise of the president,” Tennessee Lt. Gov. Randy McNally (R) writes in the [_Times Free Press_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=950669828e&e=3ee4985eea). “The president is keeping his promise with the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA). It is a measured, careful approach that stays true to free trade principles while making sure our farmers, laborers and white-collar workers are protected.”
---
|
---
| “Our cumulative trade deficit with Mexico since 1985 exceeds $1 trillion, 98 percent of which came after NAFTA went into effect. This is why we need USMCA,” Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross writes in [_The Roanoke Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=085bc9ca61&e=3ee4985eea). The new deal will finally level the playing field across American industries and will create “tens of thousands of more jobs.”
---
|
---
| “Because of the impact the agriculture industry has on Virginia’s economy, there will be no more important debate in Congress this year than the future of the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), now called the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA),” former Virginia Commerce Secretary Todd Haymore writes in the [_Richmond Times-Dispatch_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fea0cf123c&e=3ee4985eea). “Our elected representatives in Washington must be ready to fight for the passage of the USMCA as soon as possible. Failure to stabilize the United States’ relationships with Canada and Mexico will have negative consequences for Virginia farmers and the national economy at large.”
---
|
---
| “One industry that the USMCA would benefit the most within Iowa is the dairy industry,” writes Larry Shover, President of the Iowa State Diary Association, in [_The Courier_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3be0edbc71&e=3ee4985eea). Currently, U.S. dairy exports to Canada are heavily limited by the Class 7 pricing scheme and tariff-rate quotas. The USMCA tears down Class 7 pricing and requires more transparency, making the new trade deal vital for the U.S. dairy industry. “We need our members of Congress to ratify the USMCA now.”
---
|

The new deal will finally level the playing field across American industries and will create “tens of thousands of more jobs.” | | “Because of the impact the agriculture industry has on Virginia’s economy, there will be no more important debate in Congress this year than the future of the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), now called the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA),” former Virginia Commerce Secretary Todd Haymore writes in the . “Our elected representatives in Washington must be ready to fight for the passage of the USMCA as soon as possible. Failure to stabilize the United States’ relationships with Canada and Mexico will have negative consequences for Virginia farmers and the national economy at large.” | | “One industry that the USMCA would benefit the most within Iowa is the dairy industry,” writes Larry Shover, President of the Iowa State Diary Association, in . Currently, U.S. dairy exports to Canada are heavily limited by the Class 7 pricing scheme and tariff-rate quotas. The USMCA tears down Class 7 pricing and requires more transparency, making the new trade deal vital for the U.S. dairy industry. “We need our members of Congress to ratify the USMCA now.” | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

name: Cory Booker
subject: Amy McGrath can beat Mitch McConnell
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Amy McGrath. A retired fighter pilot. A Marine. A Kentuckian. **And now she 's running to replace Mitch McConnell in the halls of the Senate.** At the age of 13, Amy dreamed of becoming a fighter pilot, but women were barred from serving in combat roles in our military. So she wrote a letter to her senator asking for his help to change that law. She never heard back from Mitch McConnell. She accomplished what she set out to do anyway -- serving 20 years in the Marines where she flew 89 combat missions in Iraq and Afghanistan before retiring and moving back home to Kentucky to raise her family. Like so many Americans, she wanted a level playing field and when she was told no, she didn't stop working toward achieving it. **That 's why we need Amy in the Senate. [I'm personally asking: can you chip in $1, $5, or whatever you can to help fuel her campaign to defeat Mitch McConnell?](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190710-em1?refcode=em_fr_20190710_CB_20190710-fr-CB-AmyMcGrath-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Mitch McConnell has engaged in toxic partisan politics as the leader of Senate Republicans. He shut down the government multiple times and helped engineer the theft of a Supreme Court seat. He described blocking President Obama's judicial nominees as one of his top accomplishments. And he's been Trump's top ally in the Senate, helping drive the calls for cuts to Medicare and Social Security and repeatedly attempting to repeal the Affordable Care Act. **We desperately need new leadership in Congress.** But taking on the majority leader isn't an easy task. Amy's going up against super PACs, deep-pocketed ultra-right donors, and dark money groups who will fight hard to keep Mitch in power. I know Amy McGrath can and will be the next senator of Kentucky. But she needs our help. [**Chip in right now to split a donation with Amy McGrath. Let 's make sure Amy has the resources needed to win this seat.**](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190710-em1?refcode=em_fr_20190710_CB_20190710-fr-CB-AmyMcGrath-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Thanks,
Cory
[](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190710-em1?refcode=em_fr_20190710_CB_20190710-fr-CB-AmyMcGrath-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

| I'll be straightforward: [I'm asking you to make a contribution to my campaign, and I hope you'll give me a moment to explain why.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad84/134673f2/fcd524/699688e9/3078242053/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/clean-up-corruption?refcode=EM_FR_B1_071019_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

Many people said our campaign was a long shot. They said I had almost no chance of being in the debate. [Thanks to your support, we proved them all wrong -- but now we need to do it again.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad84/134673f2/fcd524/699688e9/3078242053/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/clean-up-corruption?refcode=EM_FR_B1_071019_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

My campaign is powered by people. I refuse to take contributions from corporate special interests, and I can't write myself a $100 MILLION check.

Every step I've taken in this journey has been because you're there with me, ensuring our campaign has what it takes to win this hard-fought race.

[It's your commitment to fueling our campaign that keeps us going. ](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad84/134673f2/fcd524/699688e9/3078242053/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/clean-up-corruption?refcode=EM_FR_B1_071019_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

Despite coming into this race later than many of the other candidates, our campaign has made incredible progress.

The next round of debates is in less than one month and the doubters are itching to "prove" that this campaign won't survive. We need to show that we're not slowing down.

[**That 's why today, I'm counting on your support again. We can't afford to fall short now that we've come so far. Will you rush a donation right now to shut down the doubters again ahead of the next round of debates? >>**](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad84/134673f2/fcd524/699688e9/3078242053/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/clean-up-corruption?refcode=EM_FR_B1_071019_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

clean_body: | | | I'll be straightforward: "" Many people said our campaign was a long shot. They said I had almost no chance of being in the debate. "" My campaign is powered by people. Every step I've taken in this journey has been because you're there with me, ensuring our campaign has what it takes to win this hard-fought race. "" Despite coming into this race later than many of the other candidates, our campaign has made incredible progress. name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: close the detention centers
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | | Friend, we've seen the horrific news stories and reports coming out of the migrant detention facilities at the border.

It's unacceptable that the Trump administration has denied detained children basic hygiene items like toothbrushes, soap, and towels -- for days on end. This cruel treatment must stop.

Let's send a message to the Trump administration. [Add your name to stand with us and demand an end to family separation and the inhumane treatment of children.](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz. "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/Close-the-Detention-Centers?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+to+stand+with+us+and+deman&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190709_1_1&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190709_1_1")

[](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz. "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/Close-the-Detention-Centers?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190709_1_1&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190709_1_1")
My colleagues and I have called for an investigation of the company that runs the Homestead detention center for migrant children here in Florida. We've also called for its closure after seeing of the center's heartbreaking, prison-like conditions that hundreds of children endure there every day -- and it's all happening while Congress is denied basic, unfettered oversight of this facility in violation of the law. But we can't stop there.

We must fight back against the Trump administration's cruel and inhumane treatment of children at detention centers along the border. It's our duty to make our voices heard now.

Children belong with their families, not in detention centers. [Add your name right away and tell the Trump administration to close the facilities!](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz. "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/Close-the-Detention-Centers?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=3+-+Add+your+name+right+away+and+tell+the+Tr&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190709_1_1&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190709_1_1")

Thanks for taking action,

Debbie

name: Paco Fabian
subject: Joe Biden called Medicare for All "irrational"
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
#
Our Revolution is holding "Ambulance Tour" events around the country in Congressional districts where representatives continue to side with the interests of the healthcare industry over the health of families they swore an oath to represent.

As we saw when Joe Biden called Medicare for All "irrational," there is no shortage of politicians willing to embarrass themselves in transparently desperate attempts to raise money from the health care and pharmaceutical industries--and all in an effort to keep up with Bernie's grassroots fundraising. **That 's why we can't afford to let up even a little bit.**

[_**Donate here to help us hold Democrats and Republicans accountable for refusing to support Medicare for All >>**_](https://go.ourrevolution. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=1+-+Donate+here+to+help+us+hold+Democrats+an&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

**\----Begin Forwarded Message----**

The health insurance and pharmaceutical industries are dead set on preventing every American from getting the healthcare we need and deserve. They spend the profits they make -- from providing us with inadequate care -- on donations to politicians of both parties.

That's why Our Revolution is fighting back with our Medicare for All "Ambulance Tour." We're traveling across the country to pressure politicians -- Republicans and Democrats -- who side with greedy corporations over the well-being of families.
[SUPPORT OUR REVOLUTION](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=2+-+SUPPORT+OUR+REVOLUTION&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

* * *
_**Below is the personal story of Anna-Marta Visky, an Our Revolution leader who recently hosted a powerful Medicare for All Ambulance Tour event in New Jersey:**_

Becoming a mother opened my eyes to the cruelty of our healthcare non-system. Shortly after my child was born, I was diagnosed with postpartum depression. But I wasn't able to focus on getting better. The co-pays for my therapy put serious financial stress on my young family.

Then, because of my family history of breast cancer, my doctor told me to get an MRI. My insurance would only cover part of the cost -- and that was AFTER I paid a $3,000 deductible. And yet, this same for-profit insurance company can afford to pay its CEO tens of millions of dollars each year.

The outrageous truth is that my healthcare struggle is far from unique. Everyone knows someone with a healthcare horror story. The damage being done on a daily basis is not just sad. It's completely avoidable.

That's why, as chair of Our Revolution in Monmouth County, New Jersey, I'm organizing to hold representatives like Andy Kim and Frank Pallone - chair of the powerful House Committee on Energy and Commerce - accountable to passing Medicare for All.
[](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")
Telling personal stories and doing direct action events are crucial to holding politicians accountable -- and we are seeing the results. After Our Revolution activists took action in deep-red Texas and Kentucky, members of Congress in those states became supporters of Medicare for All.

[That's why I'm reaching out to ask you to help Our Revolution keep shaming Republicans AND Democrats to support healthcare as a right. Can you chip in $27, or whatever your family can afford, to help us push Medicare for All into existence?](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=4+-+Thats+why+Im+reaching+out+to+ask+you+to+&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")
[DONATE $10 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=5+-+DONATE+10+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

[DONATE $20.20 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=20.20&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=6+-+DONATE+2020+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

[DONATE $27 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=7+-+DONATE+27+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

[DONATE $50 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=50&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=8+-+DONATE+50+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

[DONATE $100 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=100&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=9+-+DONATE+100+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")
After Our Revolution activists took action, Representative John Yarmuth, a Democrat from Kentucky, signed on to Medicare for All. [He said: "It's not a matter of if we are going to get single payer, it's a matter of when."](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=10+-+He+said+Its+not+a+matter+of+if+we+are+go&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

I couldn't agree more. But we also must recognize that the health insurance and drug industry lobbies will do everything they can to stop us. In order to win, we must stand together and demand Medicare for All.

Donate here to directly fund our on-the-ground activism for Medicare for All: [https://go.ourrevolution.com/medicare-for-all](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=11+-+httpsgoourrevolutioncommedicareforall&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190710_1_2")

Anna-Marta

body:
The Democratic primary shouldn't be decided by billionaires, whether they're funding Super PACs or funding themselves. If you're a billionaire, you can already buy yourself a mansion, a private... | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852eee7/45c30650/174132643/669c0cf5/698674171/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")

This week, the Democratic primary got a little more crowded -- and potentially a lot more expensive.

Tom Steyer announced that he's running for president, and that he plans to spend at least $100 million of his own money to try to win.

Look: It's good to have a competitive primary.

But here's the thing: **We need our candidates to compete to have the best ideas -- not just to write themselves the biggest checks.**

**Can you chip in to help keep our campaign 's message front and center in this race? Your support matters because Elizabeth isn't self-funding. She doesn't have a Super PAC, and she doesn't want one. name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: two years ago today!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Peter,

**Two years ago today I launched my campaign for Congress!** It's unbelievable how far we've come. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/_cN9qw0RZSoNE5xjVkQSN1g_l0WiEQl7jcz_Fxflsts=/euSYP-bzmFI3_37yB-z2KWmEKjLC_N3VwQePq7tOOr4=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190710-1-EM&recurring=TRUE&amount=25&amounts=25,50,100,250)
We held our first meeting in my living room with some of my dearest friends and earliest supporters. In these two years, we built a real grassroots campaign that listened to Central Virginians, we raised significant funds without corporate PAC money, and we defeated an entrenched opponent in a district most pundits said was wholly unwinnable.

**And we did these things together.** [](https://act.myngp.com/el/_cN9qw0RZSoNE5xjVkQSN1g_l0WiEQl7jcz_Fxflsts=/euSYP-bzmFI3_37yB-z2KWmEKjLC_N3VwQePq7tOOr4=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190710-1-EM&recurring=TRUE&amount=25&amounts=25,50,100,250)
This was my first interview! When I think back to the day I launched our campaign, I remember the excitement and nervousness about what I was endeavoring to do. I knew the race would be a long, uphill effort with unknown challenges along the way, but I also believed it would be worth it. Your support and the energy I felt from people I met throughout Central Virginia helped push me through the challenges and keep me focused on the "why" behind my decision to run.

I have fond memories of launch day and of the 2018 race, and I am proud of all that I am doing and working to achieve on behalf of the people of VA07!

**But today I 'm also thinking about 2020.**

We're going to have a challenging race, and I'll need to work just as hard to keep my seat in our R+6 district as I worked to win it in 2018! I'm excited to do just that, but I will need your help and the resources to communicate what I'm working for and why I want to continue serving in Congress. Can you help contribute to my campaign so that we can be in the best possible position?

We will bring one more supporter (and a friend) to meet Beto and attend the debate in Detroit.

body:
Across America, 37 million patients suffer from chronic kidney disease. Each year, more than 100,000 begin dialysis to... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=135ab100f3&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 10, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump refuses to accept the status quo on one of America’s deadliest diseases**

**Across America,** 37 million patients suffer from chronic kidney disease. Each year, more than 100,000 begin dialysis to treat end-stage renal disease—50 percent of whom die within five years.

**That mortality rate** is far too high. All told, kidney disease ranks as the ninth leading cause of death in America. The current quality of care for these patients is unacceptable, and the treatment options available to them are far too expensive.

**President Donald J. Trump,** as with so many other issues confronting America, has refused to accept that awful status quo. At the Ronald Reagan Building and International Trade Center in Washington today, he signed an executive order that takes the biggest step in decades toward advancing American kidney health.

**🎬****President Trump:** [It’s time to get “Americans off these waitlists.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3ca4835aad&e=3ee4985eea)

**“Today** we’re taking groundbreaking action to bring new hope to millions of Americans suffering from kidney disease,” he said.

**Under President Trump’s** new order**,** Medicare will test adjusting payment incentives to encourage preventative kidney care, as well as the use of home dialysis and kidney transplants. Furthermore, the President is directing his Administration to develop a process to get artificial kidneys to patients and expedite the process of kidney matching.

**And crucially,** the President is beginning the process of removing financial barriers to organ donation, including by allowing for the reimbursement of lost wages and childcare expenses faced by organ donors.

**“For these patients,** their loved ones, and for the impacted—all those impacted by kidney disease—I'm here to say: We are fighting by your side, and we're determined to get you the best treatment anywhere in the world,” President Trump said.

[President Trump’s executive order is a bold new approach to kidney treatment.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bc1122d770&e=3ee4985eea)

** ****President Trump:** [“We are with you every step of the way.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cff2b0586b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Ivanka Trump helps secure women’s rights legislation in Ivory Coast**

**In April,** Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump traveled to Africa, where she made the case for advancing women’s rights and legal status across the world. One of the countries she visited, Ivory Coast, today took concrete steps toward that vision by passing legislation encouraged by Ms. Trump.

**“We are pleased** to recognize and applaud the Ivorian government’s recent passage of the marriage law, which supports women’s equal management of household assets,” she said in a statement to _The Associated Press_.

**This new legislation** paves the way for the economic empowerment of women and sets an example for other countries to follow. Specifically, the updated family code makes the social and political landscape more equitable for women by getting rid of laws that prevented them from owning or inheriting property.

**Reforms such as these** are a top priority for the White House’s Women’s Global Development and Prosperity Initiative (W-GDP), which Ivanka Trump helps lead. W-GDP’s core message is that when women are empowered, both their families and their communities benefit substantially. Launched in February, W-GDP aims to empower 50 million women throughout the world by 2025.

**Today,** U.S. International Development Agency Administrator Mark Green, alongside Ms. Trump, announced 14 new projects in support of W-GDP. These partnerships alone will leverage millions of dollars to reach more than 100,000 women in the years ahead.

[_The Associated Press_ : Ivory Coast passes legislation encouraged by Ivanka Trump](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=671587b9a1&e=3ee4985eea)

**Learn more:** [President Trump’s W-GDP aims to empower 50 million women ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=67d71ca5b5&e=3ee4985eea)
